rationale: The citation is treated in a dedicated paragraph where the authors summarize Chen and Lim (2017)'s specific result that, in team contests with heterogeneous abilities, the average metric yields higher effort than maximum or minimum. They use that result as a direct comparison point to motivate their own contribution, explaining that they shift to group-based commissions and a different heterogeneity structure. They then interpret their opposite focal result (maximum outperforming average) as a meaningful extension to a different incentive setting rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Chen and Lim (2017) by carrying the team-output-metric question into group-based commission contracts instead of team contests. It contrasts model assumptions and empirical tests with the cited study and shows that the performance ranking of average versus maximum can reverse in this new context.
